meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Why has Belfast erupted in anti-immigrant violence after a knife attack?
items.summary: Ministers have blamed far-right online agitators for stoking racial tension following a stabbing.

items.title: Who pays for a supersized World Cup?
items.summary: The biggest World Cup ever, with 48 teams and 104 matches, is expected to bring a record $13bn for FIFA.

items.title: Graham Platner wins Maine primary election: Results and key takeaways
items.summary: Platner wins in Maine, Republican Nancy Mace loses in S Carolina. Results for Nevada and North Carolina also announced.

items.title: A four-year-old’s recovery from the trauma of war in Lebanon
items.summary: Four-year-old Malika was seriously wounded in an Israeli attack that killed her mother.

items.title: Why is the FIFA World Cup 2026 being held in three countries?
items.summary: The 2026 World Cup is the first to be held in three different host nations - but why? Al Jazeera explains.

items.title: Iran fires missiles at US bases carrying images of killed commanders
items.summary: Iranian state media released video showing the launch of missiles towards US bases in the Middle East.

items.title: India’s Bengal pushes out Muslim Bangladeshis, deepening religious tensions
items.summary: Hundreds 'pushed' to border and many others put in detention centres as part of a crackdown on undocumented migrants.

items.title: Argentina World Cup 2026 preview: Players to watch, group matches and squad
items.summary: Messi is hoping to overcome injury to help Argentina become the first back-to-back World Cup champions since 1962.

items.title: Anti-immigration protests break out in Belfast after knife attack
items.summary: Northern Ireland’s political leaders call for calm after a knife attack led to anti-immigration protests in the capital.

items.title: Rising from the rubble: A Gaza tech incubator defies Israel’s war
items.summary: Israel's war on Gaza has decimated the tech sector, killing experts and destroying incubators that offered opportunity.

items.title: Spidercam crashes onto pitch during Hungary match
items.summary: A spider camera crashed near the touchline during a football friendly between Hungary and Kazakhstan.

items.title: Twelve killed in Johannesburg mass shooting, manhunt under way
items.summary: Police say a manhunt is under way for more than 10 suspects after the attack on an informal settlement.

items.title: Baltic states fear Russia-Ukraine war spillover after drone incursions
items.summary: A spate of drone incursions fuels anxieties about a possible military conflict with Russia.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 explained in maps and charts
items.summary: Qualified teams, groups, schedule and snapshot of stadiums that will host World Cup matches.

items.title: Explosion in Bahrain as Iran attacks US bases
items.summary: CCTV footage shows an explosion in Bahrain’s capital Manama after what authorities say was an Iranian missile strike.

items.title: Messi comes off the bench to score in Argentina’s final World Cup warm-up
items.summary: Argentina ease past Iceland in their final friendly before the World Cup, winning 3-0 in Auburn, Alabama.

items.title: Ukrainian drones strike Sevastopol museum and key Russian oil refineries
items.summary: The multiday campaign has killed a rail worker, closed the bombed Chongar road bridge and caused a fuel crisis

items.title: Iran war day 103: US strikes after helicopter shot down, Tehran hits back
items.summary: Analysts warn of further escalation after tit-for-tat attacks between Tehran and Washington.

items.title: Indonesia jails four military officers for acid attack on rights activist
items.summary: Prosecutors argued in court that the soldiers were motivated by anger over Yunus’s activism.

items.title: Prediction platform Kalshi to collect job details to combat insider trading
items.summary: Prediction platform says it will seek employment information for markets at 'heightened' risk of manipulation.

items.title: Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate primary, vows to oust Collins
items.summary: Platner wins Maine Senate primary despite controversies, setting up a key race against Republican Susan Collins.

items.title: Trita Parsi: Iran is pursuing a doctrine of swift retaliation
items.summary: Iran has abandoned strategic patience for a new doctrine: swift, severe retaliation against any US attack.

items.title: Pope Leo welcomed in Barcelona with traditional Catalan human tower
items.summary: More than 100 members of the Castellers de Vilafranca built a 10-metre-high human tower to welcome Pope Leo

items.title: Pakistan claims killing 26 Afghan fighters; Taliban says civilians killed
items.summary: Pakistan targets TTP fighters across Afghan border, as the Taliban says 11 children among those killed in air attacks.

items.title: UN report: Israeli forces shield settlers during attacks on Palestinians
items.summary: The UN says Israeli security forces routinely accompany settlers and act as a shield for their violence.
